code:
import collections

class Solution:
    def constrainedSubsetSum(self, nums: list[int], k: int) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        
        # dp[i] will store the maximum sum of a subsequence ending at nums[i]
        # satisfying the constraint.
        dp = [0] * n
        
        # Deque to maintain a monotonic decreasing sequence of dp values
        # (indices of elements in the window [i-k, i-1])
        # The front of the deque will always have the index corresponding to the maximum dp value
        # in the current window.
        dq = collections.deque() 

        for i in range(n):
            # 1. Remove elements from the front of the deque whose indices are
            # no longer within the valid window [i-k, i-1].
            while dq and dq[0] < i - k:
                dq.popleft()

            # 2. Calculate dp[i].
            # The current element nums[i] can either start a new subsequence
            # (effectively adding 0 to nums[i]) or extend a previous valid subsequence.
            # We choose the maximum among these options.
            # If dq is empty, it means no valid previous element within the window,
            # so we only consider starting a new subsequence.
            current_max_prev_dp = 0
            if dq:
                current_max_prev_dp = max(0, dp[dq[0]]) 
            
            dp[i] = nums[i] + current_max_prev_dp

            # 3. Maintain the monotonic decreasing property of the deque.
            # Remove elements from the back if their dp value is less than or equal to dp[i].
            # This is because dp[i] is a better candidate for future calculations:
            # it has a greater or equal sum and is closer to future indices.
            while dq and dp[dq[-1]] <= dp[i]:
                dq.pop()
            
            # 4. Add the current index to the back of the deque.
            dq.append(i)

        # The result is the maximum value found in the dp array,
        # as the optimal subsequence could end at any position.
        return max(dp)
prompt: Constrained subsequence sum using deque yaz.
